monsters.passive_ability: Tabi-Tabi Po Ward — The first direct attack against Nuno has reduced damage. Allies who use Guard gain minor Spirit Defense.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Mound Elder's Rebuke — Strikes one enemy with ancestral earth energy, dealing Earth/Ancestor damage and applying Weakened.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Tabi-Tabi Circle — Creates a protective mound circle that grants Shielded to allies and may Root enemies who enter.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Fortify, Earth Spike, Root Prison, Spirit Ward, Ancestral Guard, Cleanse, Taunt, Stone Skin, Weaken, Barrier, Calm, Counter Ready, Protect Ally, Blessing
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium-Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires repairing a disturbed mound and asking permission properly
monsters.habitat: Rice Terraces, Balete Grove, Ancestral House, Sacred Shrine, Highland Village
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Rice grains, betel nut, clean water, small candle, whispered apology
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Nuno sa Punso is one of the most familiar land spirits in everyday Filipino belief. People are taught to say 'tabi-tabi po' when passing mounds, roots, or hidden places to avoid offending unseen residents. In ALAMAT, the Nuno are elder guardians of small territories: anthills, soil mounds, boundary stones, old roots, and forgotten corners of family land. This Nuno becomes angry after children flatten its mound while adults laugh at the warning. The result is not simple punishment but imbalance: tools go missing, ankles twist near the field, and the soil refuses to hold seedlings. To earn trust, the player must rebuild the mound, teach the children respect without fear, and make a humble offering. Once bonded, Nuno becomes a defensive guardian focused on wards, roots, ancestral protection, and teaching the party that small places can hold great power.
monsters.folklore_summary: Nuno sa Punso is a dwarf-like or elder spirit believed to live in anthills or mounds. Disturbing its home without asking permission is said to cause illness, swelling, or misfortune.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Nuno as a respected boundary elder whose power is strongest in small places people ignore.
monsters.physical_description: A tiny ancient elder with bark-brown skin, white moss-like beard, woven leaf cloak, small staff made from root and stone, glowing green-gold eyes, and a mound-shaped aura around its feet.
monsters.weaknesses: Disrespectful noise, broken mounds, Fire, forced relocation, corruption in soil
monsters.resistances: Earth, Ancestor, Root, Fear, Blind, forced movement
monsters.npc_related: Lola Ines; Tala the child; Apo Raniag the boundary keeper
monsters.boss_variant: Nuno ng Pitong Punso — controls seven mound wards across the battlefield.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Nuno ng Galit na Lupa — curses every step and turns roots into traps.
monsters.divine_variant: Apo ng Munting Hangganan — radiant elder guardian of respected boundaries.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A tiny elder voice clearing its throat, followed by soil shifting and a respectful whisper.
monsters.drop_items: Mound Soil, Elder Root Staff Chip, Boundary Pebble, Tabi-Tabi Charm
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Totem, Anting-Anting, Herbal Bundle, Bell, Ancestral Charm
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Nuno sa Punso inspired by Filipino folklore: tiny elderly mound spirit, moss-white beard, bark-brown skin, leaf cloak, root-and-stone staff, small earthen mound aura, glowing green-gold eyes, transparent background, readable chibi RPG guardian style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Nuno sa Punso, Filipino mound elder spirit, tiny wise guardian, moss beard, root staff, anthill-like mound, rice field and old roots background, respectful mystical RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Nuno taps its small staff, seven earth rings ripple outward, allies receive mound-shaped shields, and roots snap at enemies who step too close.
monsters.design_notes: Important early-game lesson creature. Reinforces respect, permission, and stewardship.

monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Pan-Philippine household spirit folklore with Spanish-era duende influence
monsters.mythological_category: Espiritu / household and earth little folk
monsters.passive_ability: Little House Luck — Grants minor Luck to allies. If treated respectfully in a quest, shop prices and gathering rates may improve locally.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Tiny Hammer Blessing — Repairs ally armor with earth magic, granting Fortified and minor healing.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Underfloor Mischief — Sends small unseen helpers under enemy feet, applying Confusion and Slow.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Haste, Confuse, Earth Spike, Blessing, Cleanse, Fortify, Barrier, Trick Path, Stone Throw, Calm, Spirit Ward, Weaken, Evasive Step, Ancestral Guard
monsters.capture_difficulty: Easy-Medium / Trust Difficulty: Requires leaving a respectful house offering and fixing a small broken object
monsters.habitat: Ancestral House, Market Town, Rice Terraces, Highland Village, Sacred Shrine
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Milk, rice cakes, tiny cup of coffee, repaired toy, small coin
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Duwende are little folk often said to live in houses, mounds, corners, gardens, or under floors. Some help humans; others punish rudeness, theft, and carelessness. In ALAMAT, Duwende are the tiny maintainers of ordinary spaces. They tighten loose nails, hide dangerous tools from children, move lost coins toward those who need them, and play pranks on people who never say thank you. This Duwende has turned a household upside down after its favorite toy-house was broken and blamed on a child. To earn trust, the player must identify the real culprit, repair the toy-house, and leave a tiny meal without trying to see the Duwende eat. Once bonded, Duwende becomes a support Nilalang that improves luck, repairs defenses, and causes small but useful battlefield disruptions.
monsters.folklore_summary: Duwende are small supernatural beings in Filipino folklore, often connected to houses, mounds, luck, mischief, and everyday household interactions. The term is related to Spanish duende but has localized Filipino meanings.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT treats Duwende as small domestic and earth spirits who maintain the unseen order of homes and everyday places.
monsters.physical_description: A tiny humanoid spirit wearing a woven cap, patched vest, bead belt, and small tool pouch. It has bright eyes, earth-smudged cheeks, oversized slippers, and a lantern no larger than a firefly.
monsters.weaknesses: Disrespect, iron clutter, loud insults, broken offerings, anti-trickster bells
monsters.resistances: Earth, Trickster, Confusion, Slow, small-object hazards
monsters.npc_related: Lola Ines; Jun the carpenter's grandson; Nene the blamed child
monsters.boss_variant: Duwende Maestro ng Bahay — summons tiny tools and furniture traps.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Duwendeng Masama ang Loob — steals luck and turns household objects hostile.
monsters.divine_variant: Munting Tagapag-ayos — blessed little helper that repairs broken sanctuaries.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A tiny proud squeak followed by tool taps and a mischievous giggle.
monsters.drop_items: Tiny Coin, Duwende Tool, Firefly Lantern, Patched Cap Thread
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bell, Beadwork, Woven Garb, Totem, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Duwende inspired by Filipino folklore: tiny house-earth spirit, woven cap, patched vest, small tool pouch, oversized slippers, bright eyes, tiny firefly lantern, transparent background, readable chibi RPG support creature style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Filipino Duwende, tiny household spirit with woven cap, patched clothes, tool pouch, bright mischievous eyes, underfloor house corner background, warm magical RPG art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Duwende whistles, tiny invisible helpers tap hammers under the floor, ally armor glows with repaired seams, then enemy feet are tripped by little earth bumps.
monsters.design_notes: Good early common companion. Useful for tutorial systems and household quests.

monsters.passive_ability: Petal Nurse — Healing skills restore a small additional amount if the target has no harmful status effects.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Sampaguita Glow — Releases a fragrant burst of light that heals one ally and grants Hopeful.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Butterfly Halo Dance — Summons a ring of butterflies that cleanses allies and blinds enemies with soft sunlight.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heal, Cleanse, Life Bloom, Leaf Storm, Haste, Blessing, Calm, Regeneration, Barrier, Sacred Light, Reveal, Spirit Ward, Hopeful Hymn, Evasive Flight, Purify
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium / Trust Difficulty: Requires restoring flower patches and avoiding harm to butterflies
monsters.habitat: Balete Grove, Waterfall Sanctuary, Rice Terraces, Sacred Shrine, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Sampaguita flowers, honey drop, clean water, tiny woven basket, gentle song
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Lambana are small nature spirits often imagined as graceful, bright, and closely tied to flowers, trees, and gentle places. In ALAMAT, Lambana are caretakers of delicate life: seedlings, butterflies, flower patches, dew, and the quiet healing that happens after storms. This Lambana appears when a shrine garden stops blooming because children have been catching every butterfly for trade. The player must free the butterflies, replant flowers, and teach the village a festival game that celebrates beauty without harming it. Once bonded, Lambana becomes a fast healer-support companion. It is fragile, but it excels at cleansing, hope, and small healing effects that keep allies alive over time.
monsters.folklore_summary: Lambana are often described in Filipino folklore as small fairy-like nature spirits associated with beauty, forests, flowers, and magical places.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT makes Lambana the small caretakers of fragile natural beauty and recovery after harm.
monsters.physical_description: A tiny luminous humanoid with butterfly-like wings, sampaguita petal clothing, dark hair tied with flower buds, golden-green eyes, and soft sunlight dust trailing from its hands.
monsters.weaknesses: Poison, heavy smoke, Silence, careless handling of flowers, corrupted gardens
monsters.resistances: Forest, Spirit, Sun, Fear, Despair, minor poison after cleansing
monsters.npc_related: Lina the flower gatherer; Tala the forest child; Apo Marikit the shrine caretaker
monsters.boss_variant: Lambana Reyna ng Munting Hardin — summons healing flowers and butterfly shields.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Lambanang Lanta — withered fairy that spreads Despair pollen.
monsters.divine_variant: Liwanag ng Sampaguita — radiant flower guardian with golden butterfly wings.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A bright bell-like giggle mixed with butterfly wings and a tiny sung note.
monsters.drop_items: Sampaguita Petal, Butterfly Dust, Dew Pearl, Tiny Flower Thread
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Herbal Bundle, Beadwork, Woven Garb, Instrument, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Lambana inspired by Filipino folklore: tiny fairy-like nature spirit, butterfly wings, sampaguita petal clothing, dark hair with flower buds, golden-green eyes, sunlight dust, transparent background, readable chibi RPG healer style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Lambana, Filipino flower fairy spirit, butterfly wings, sampaguita petals, warm sunlight, tiny gentle face, shrine garden background, delicate RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Lambana spins through the air, butterflies form a glowing halo, sampaguita petals burst outward, allies are healed, and enemies are briefly blinded by soft sunlight.
monsters.design_notes: Small, gentle, and useful. Strong contrast against horror creatures in the Codex.

monsters.passive_ability: Hidden Court Presence — Gains Magic and Spirit Defense while Hidden, Mist, or Spirit Glow is active.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Enchanted Court Decree — Commands spirit energy against one enemy, dealing Spirit damage and applying Silence.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Palace Behind the Trees — Opens a temporary hidden-realm gate, granting allies Barrier while confusing enemies.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Spirit Bolt, Barrier, Silence, Confuse, Moon Veil, Mist Step, Haste, Blessing, Reveal, Spirit Ward, Cleanse, Dark Pulse, Calm, Reflect, Mana Burn
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires observing etiquette in the hidden court and refusing a false bargain
monsters.habitat: Spirit Threshold, Balete Grove, Hidden Lake, Waterfall Sanctuary, Ancestral House
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Fine cloth, respectful greeting, fragrant rice, flower wine, silver leaf charm
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Engkanto are enchanted beings said to live in hidden realms near forests, rivers, trees, and places where the ordinary world feels slightly too beautiful or strange. In ALAMAT, Engkanto society is organized into hidden courts that remember old bargains between humans and spirits. They are not automatically benevolent or cruel; they are formal, proud, and dangerous to those who ignore etiquette. This Engkanto appears when a young musician vanishes after accepting food from a silver-lit feast beneath a tree. To earn trust, the player must enter the hidden court, speak with proper respect, reject a tempting bargain, and resolve the insult that trapped the musician. Once bonded, Engkanto becomes a high-magic support mage using barriers, silence, confusion, and hidden-realm gates.
monsters.folklore_summary: Engkanto are enchanted beings in Filipino folklore, often described as beautiful or otherworldly spirits living in hidden realms. Stories warn humans to treat them with respect and avoid careless bargains or offense.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Engkanto as noble hidden-realm spirits whose power is tied to etiquette, beauty, bargains, and the danger of disrespect.
monsters.physical_description: An elegant otherworldly noble with luminous skin, long dark hair, embroidered translucent clothing, silver leaf ornaments, faintly glowing eyes, and a misty palace silhouette visible behind them.
monsters.weaknesses: Broken etiquette, iron bells, Sun truth rites, refusal of false bargains, name-binding rituals
monsters.resistances: Spirit, Mist, Divine, Charm, Confusion, Silence, magical damage
monsters.npc_related: Amaya the musician; Bai Amirah the cloth artisan; Apo Raniag the etiquette keeper
monsters.boss_variant: Engkanto Prinsipe ng Nakatagong Palasyo — rewrites battle rules through court decrees.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Engkanto ng Huwad na Kasunduan — traps allies through cursed bargains.
monsters.divine_variant: Maharlika ng Liwanag na Korte — radiant noble who protects honest agreements.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A calm beautiful voice speaking a formal greeting, followed by silver chimes and distant court murmurs.
monsters.drop_items: Silver Leaf, Enchanted Cloth, Court Seal, Hidden Feast Grain
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Beadwork, Woven Garb, Bell, Spirit Mask, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of an Engkanto inspired by Filipino folklore: elegant hidden-realm noble spirit, luminous skin, long dark hair, translucent embroidered clothing, silver leaf ornaments, misty palace aura, transparent background, readable chibi RPG mage silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of an Engkanto, Filipino enchanted hidden-realm noble, luminous face, silver leaf ornaments, elegant translucent clothing, misty forest palace background, beautiful and dangerous RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Engkanto raises a hand like issuing a royal decree, silver script fills the air, a hidden palace gate opens behind the party, allies gain barriers while enemies hear confusing court whispers.
monsters.design_notes: Useful for etiquette quests and bargain mechanics. Avoid making all Engkanto villains.

monsters.province_origin: Widely present in indigenous Philippine spiritual traditions
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Multiple indigenous and regional Philippine traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Remembered Name — When an ally falls below half HP, Anito grants a small shield once per battle if the party has completed a remembrance quest.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Voice of the Ancestors — Calls ancestral echoes to heal allies and increase Faith.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Old Name Judgment — Speaks a forgotten name against enemies, dealing Ancestor/Spirit damage and applying Weakened or Fear.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Ancestral Guard, Spirit Ward, Heal, Blessing, Cleanse, Sacred Light, Barrier, Calm, Hopeful Hymn, Reveal, Spirit Bolt, Fortify, Purify, Counter Ready, Regeneration
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires recovering the spirit's true name and honoring descendants
monsters.habitat: Sacred Shrine, Ancestral House, Rice Terraces, Highland Village, Spirit Threshold
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Rice, candle, heirloom cloth, remembered name, clean water
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Anito is a broad and important concept tied to ancestor spirits, nature spirits, and unseen presences in many Philippine traditions. In ALAMAT, Anito are not a single species but a sacred order of remembered beings who continue to protect families, places, and vows. This Anito appears as an echo around an ancestral house whose descendants have forgotten the name of the one who built it. Without remembrance, the house loses its protective warmth and becomes vulnerable to corruption. To earn trust, the player must gather fragments of the ancestor's life: a woven cloth, a planting song, a witness account, and the true name erased from a record. Once bonded, Anito becomes a shaman-support Nilalang that protects allies through memory, faith, ancestral shields, and old-name judgment.
monsters.folklore_summary: Anito refers to ancestor spirits, nature spirits, and spiritual presences in various indigenous Philippine belief systems. Meanings differ across cultures and contexts, and the term carries deep historical and religious significance.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT treats Anito respectfully as remembered spirits tied to names, lineage, land, and protective vows, while keeping the game world fictional.
monsters.physical_description: A luminous ancestral figure partly formed from woven cloth, smoke, and warm gold light, with gentle eyes, floating heirloom objects, rice-grain sparkles, and a face that shifts subtly between generations.
monsters.weaknesses: Forgotten names, broken lineage vows, corruption of memory, disrespectful rituals, Silence
monsters.resistances: Ancestor, Spirit, Sacred, Fear, Despair, Curse, Doom
monsters.npc_related: Lola Ines; Apo Raniag; Elena the descendant; Datu Salin
monsters.boss_variant: Anito ng Nawalang Talaangkanan — tests the party with memory trials and ancestral echoes.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Anitong Nakalimutan — forgotten spirit that spreads Despair and drains Faith.
monsters.divine_variant: Anito ng Liwanag ng Lahi — radiant protector of remembered names and restored families.
monsters.cry_voice_description: Many soft elder voices speaking one name together, followed by a warm chime.
monsters.drop_items: Ancestral Thread, Remembered Name Shard, Heirloom Dust, Warm Candle Wax
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Ancestral Charm, Anting-Anting, Woven Garb, Bell, Totem
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of an Anito inspired by Philippine ancestral spirit traditions: luminous ancestor figure made of warm gold light, woven cloth, smoke, floating heirloom objects, rice-grain sparkles, shifting gentle face, transparent background, readable chibi RPG shaman style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of an Anito, Philippine ancestral spirit, warm gold aura, woven cloth, floating heirlooms, gentle multi-generational face, ancestral house and rice field background, respectful sacred RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Anito opens its hands, old names appear as golden script, ancestor silhouettes gather behind allies, shields form from woven patterns, then forgotten names strike enemies like soft thunder.
monsters.design_notes: Handle respectfully. Clearly distinguish ALAMAT fiction from real indigenous spiritual traditions.
